mainEntity.name: Is Pacific Cross a legitimate insurance provider?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Pacific Cross is registered with the Insurance Commission of the Philippines and has been operating since 1995. They are a legitimate non-life insurance provider for health coverage with over 1,600 accredited doctors and hospitals nationwide.

mainEntity.name: How much does Pacific Cross FlexiShield cost per month?

acceptedAnswer.text: Monthly premiums range from approximately ₱1,500 for basic coverage to ₱10,000+ for premium plans. The exact cost depends on your age, health history, and chosen benefit limit. Basic plans start at ₱100,000 annual benefit, while Elite plans offer ₱1,000,000+ coverage.

mainEntity.name: Can I use Pacific Cross at any hospital?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cashless hospitalization is available at accredited hospitals only (1,600+ nationwide). At non-accredited hospitals, you'll need to pay upfront and file for reimbursement, which typically takes 2-3 weeks to process.

mainEntity.name: What happens if I have a pre-existing condition?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pre-existing conditions are covered after a 6-12 month waiting period. You must disclose all conditions during application. Non-disclosure can result in claim denial and policy cancellation, even for unrelated conditions.

mainEntity.name: Will my premium increase after I file a claim?

acceptedAnswer.text: Possibly. Pacific Cross may apply premium loading based on your claims history, which is standard practice for indemnity insurance. Renewal premiums can increase by 20-50% after significant claims. It's important to budget for potential increases.

mainEntity.name: Can I stack Pacific Cross with PhilHealth and an HMO?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, and this is recommended for maximum protection. PhilHealth covers the base amount (30-50% of hospital bill), HMO handles routine care and remaining balance, while Pacific Cross provides catastrophic coverage and portability when you change jobs.
